quotes.text: People ask me what I was thinking on that last lap and honestly I wasn't thinking anything coherent. It's more like your hands are just ahead of your brain the whole time, and you only catch up after you've already won or already crashed.
quotes.speaker: Lewis Hamilton
quotes.context: Interview with BBC Sport following the 2019 British Grand Prix at Silverstone
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Hamilton really does talk about the dissociation between instinct and conscious thought in racing, and the phrasing sounds like his reflective post-race tone. The subtle giveaway is that 'already won or already crashed' is just a touch too balanced and quotable for genuinely off-the-cuff speech.

quotes.text: Baseball has been good to me since I quit trying to play it.
quotes.speaker: Whitey Herzog
quotes.context: The Hall of Fame manager reflecting on his so-so playing career versus his success as a skipper.

source.title: ESPN Page 2 — My 5 top quotes by Hall of Famer Whitey Herzog

quotes.text: Kobe used to call me at hours of the night that I can't repeat in polite company, just to argue about footwork. Not ask about it — argue. He had already decided he was right. The call was a formality.
quotes.speaker: Pau Gasol
quotes.context: Segment on ESPN's 'The Jump' with Rachel Nichols, aired February 2020 as part of the network's Kobe Bryant tribute coverage
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Gasol has shared many warm, textured anecdotes about Kobe's obsessive competitiveness and their close friendship, so this sits entirely within the plausible range. The tell is 'The call was a formality,' which is a little too epigrammatic for someone speaking in their second language.

quotes.text: I've been asked to describe what it feels like to bowl a perfect game and the honest answer is that around the seventh or eighth frame you stop being a person and become something closer to a very focused machine, and then right after the tenth frame you become a person again and you're starving.
quotes.speaker: Pete Weber
quotes.context: Interview on the PBA Tour's official podcast 'Bowling This Week,' recorded ahead of the 2015 US Open
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Weber is famously emotional and self-aware in interviews, and this blend of intense athletic description followed by a mundane physical detail is very true to his personality. The subtle giveaway is that the 'starving' punchline is a touch too deliberate — real bowlers in the moment rarely land the joke that cleanly.

quotes.text: They want Messi to stay in the tournament
quotes.speaker: Hossam Hassan
quotes.context: Egypt manager after his side's dramatic World Cup loss to Argentina

source.title: Sports — Egypt manager Hassan to switch off World Cup after Argentina ‘injustice’

quotes.text: LeBron watched more film than anybody I ever coached, and I mean that as a pure statement of fact, not a compliment, because he would come in the next day and correct ME on something I missed. That stopped being flattering around game three.
quotes.speaker: Tyronn Lue
quotes.context: The Athletic podcast 'The Lowe Post' with Zach Lowe, recorded during the 2020 NBA Finals bubble
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Lue has spoken openly about LeBron's film obsession and their candid coaching relationship, so this lands in familiar territory. The subtle tell is the turn at the end: 'That stopped being flattering around game three' is a little too well-timed to be spontaneous.
